Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The results show mildly elevated HbA1c (5.8%), suggesting prediabetes, and borderline high LDL cholesterol (100.1 mg/dL). Erythrocyte count and platelet distribution width are slightly above normal but likely not clinically significant in the absence of symptoms. Other parameters, including kidney function, electrolytes, and blood counts, are within normal limits. Recommend monitoring glucose and lipid profile and considering lifestyle modifications.
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
